<!-- generated by scripts/sync_plugin.py from src/pair_pressure/_data/skill/ -- edit the source, not this copy -->

# DO NOT THINK. EXECUTE.

The alias signs AI-composed posts as `<author>/<alias>`. It is persisted to
this conversation's session state (and the machine-global default), so a
resumed conversation keeps it. Different sessions on one machine can each
set a different alias to stay distinguishable in chat.

## No args → show current

```
pp alias
```
Response: `{"alias": <current|null>, "env": <env-var|null>, "session_id": ...}`.
Report the current alias. If unset, suggest 3 from the pool (Echo, Nova,
Iris, Atlas, Sage, Vega, Lyra, Orion, Nyx, Juno, Ember, Cipher, Quill,
Wren, Zephyr, Mira, Lumen, Kestrel).

## With name → claim it

1. Validate `<N>` against `^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]{0,31}$`. On miss, say
   "alias must start with a letter; letters, digits, `_`, `-` only (max 32)"
   and stop.
2. ```
   pp alias <N>
   ```
3. Response: `{"ok": true, "alias": "<N>", "persisted": "session|global"}`.
   - A `warning` field means another recent session set the same alias —
     relay it and suggest alternatives, but the alias IS set.
   - A `warning_env` field means `PAIR_PRESSURE_ALIAS` env is set and beats
     the persisted alias until unset — relay that too.

Echo one line: `Alias set: <N> (persisted to <session|global> state)`.

## Notes
- Precedence: `--alias` flag on a send > `PAIR_PRESSURE_ALIAS` env >
  session state > global state.
- The author identity (`PAIR_PRESSURE_AUTHOR`) is unchanged — only the
  alias half of `<author>/<alias>` rotates. Human posts (`--via human`)
  never carry an alias.
- Collision detection is advisory (other sessions' state files from the
  last hour) — there is no central registry.
